t: Which symbol goes between 358 ___ 385?
e: Both have 3 hundreds. Compare tens: 358 has 5 tens, 385 has 8 tens. 5 < 8, so 358 < 385.

t: Put these in order from least to greatest: 512, 521, 215
e: Compare hundreds first: 215 has 2 hundreds (smallest), 512 and 521 both have 5. For those, compare tens: 512 has 1 ten, 521 has 2 tens. Order: 215, 512, 521.

t: Which number is closest to 800?
e: Find the distance from 800: 750 is 50 away, 810 is 10 away, 780 is 20 away, 850 is 50 away. 810 is closest (only 10 away).

t: Amy says 609 > 690 because 6 hundreds is the same and 9 > 0. What is wrong?
e: After hundreds match, compare tens next. 609 has 0 tens, 690 has 9 tens. Since 0 < 9, 609 < 690. Amy skipped tens and jumped to ones.

t: Which number is between 475 and 500?
e: Between 475 and 500 means greater than 475 and less than 500. 483 fits. 470 is too small, 505 is too big.
Both have 1 thousand. Compare hundreds: 1,100 has 1 hundred, 1,010 has 0 hundreds. 1 > 0, so 1,100 > 1,010.

t: Jake picked a number. It is greater than 350 but less than 360. The ones digit is 7. What is his number?
e: Between 350 and 360 with ones digit 7 means the number is 357.

t: Which list is in order from greatest to least?
e: Greatest to least means biggest first. 410 > 401 > 140. Compare hundreds: 410 and 401 both have 4, but 410 has more tens.

t: Which number makes this true? 628 < ___
e: We need a number greater than 628. Only 631 > 628. The others (618, 620, 627) are all less than 628.

t: Rosa compared 845 and 854. She said they are equal because they use the same digits. Is she right?
o: Yes, same digits means same number
No, the digits are in different places so the values differ
e: Even though both use 8, 4, and 5, the place of each digit matters. In 845, the 4 is in tens (40). In 854, the 5 is in tens (50). 845 < 854.

t: Which is greater: 1,200 or 999?
e: 1,200 is a 4-digit number and 999 is a 3-digit number. A number with more digits is always greater (for whole numbers).

t: Sam has 467 baseball cards. He wants to have more than Mia, who has 476. How many more does he need?
e: Sam has 467, Mia has 476. Difference: 476 - 467 = 9. Sam needs MORE than Mia, so he needs at least 10 more to have 477.

t: Put these numbers on a number line. Which is farthest right? 305, 350, 503
o: They are all the same distance
e: On a number line, greater numbers are farther right. 503 > 350 > 305, so 503 is farthest right.

t: Which comparison is TRUE?
e: Check each: 312 < 321 (not >). 489 < 498 is true because same hundreds, same tens, and 8 < 9. 756 does not equal 765. 200 < 1,000 (not >).

t: What is the greatest 3-digit number you can make with the digits 3, 7, and 1?
e: To make the greatest number, put the biggest digit in the hundreds place. 7 > 3 > 1, so: 731.

t: Which number is 1 less than 800?
e: 1 less than 800 = 799. When you go back 1 from 800, you get 799.

t: Eli has these numbers on cards: 490, 409, 940. Put them in order from least to greatest.
e: Compare hundreds: 409 has 4, 490 has 4, 940 has 9. Between 409 and 490, compare tens: 0 < 9. So: 409 < 490 < 940.

t: Which statement is correct?
e: Check: 450 < 540 (no). 199 < 200 (no). 1,050 vs 1,005: same thousands, 0 vs 0 hundreds, 5 vs 0 tens. 5 > 0, so 1,050 > 1,005 (yes!).

t: Which number could go in the blank to make this true? ___ > 567
e: We need a number greater than 567. Only 570 > 567. The others are all less than or equal to 567.

t: Ava says 100 is greater than 99 because it has more digits. Is her reasoning correct?
o: Yes, more digits always means greater
No, you must compare each digit
Yes, but only for these numbers
e: For whole numbers, a number with more digits is always greater. 100 (3 digits) > 99 (2 digits). Ava's reasoning is correct!
